skill.name: firebase
skill.description: Firebase gives you a complete backend in minutes - auth, database, storage, functions, hosting. But the ease of setup hides real complexity. Security rules are your last line of defense, and they're easy to get wrong.
skill.summary: Expert Firebase development guidance for building secure, scalable apps with Firestore, Auth, and Cloud Functions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and weak_crypto patterns, but manual review confirms these are false positives. The backtick syntax on line 57 is markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm flags on lines 3, 15, 39, 45, 60 are triggered by generic words like 'security' and 'design', not actual weak crypto usage. This is a legitimate Firebase development skill with no security issues.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick syntax at SKILL.md:57 as Ruby/shell command execution. Manual review confirms these are markdown inline code formatting backticks (`nextjs-app-router`), not command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are used for inline code formatting. The context shows a list of related skills, not shell commands.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines. Manual review shows these are false positives triggered by generic words like 'security', 'design', and 'Firebase' - no actual cryptographic algorithms are present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain generic development terms like 'security rules', 'design', and 'Firebase'. No cryptographic algorithms are used or referenced in this skill.

content.value_statement: Firebase simplifies backend development but security rules and data modeling require expertise to avoid breaches and cost overruns. This skill provides expert guidance on Firebase best practices.
actual_capabilities: Generate Firestore security rules that properly protect data
Design NoSQL data models optimized for query patterns
Implement Firebase Authentication with OAuth and custom claims
Write Cloud Functions for backend operations and triggers
Set up Firebase Emulators for local development and testing
Optimize Firestore listeners to prevent unexpected costs
limitations: Cannot provision Firebase projects or manage billing
Cannot write complete application code without specific requirements
Does not replace official Firebase documentation for API details
May not cover niche Firebase services like Remote Config in depth

use_cases.title: Secure a new Firestore database
use_cases.description: Generate production-ready security rules for a user-owned data collection with proper read/write permissions

use_cases.title: Model social app data
use_cases.description: Design Firestore collections and queries for a social feed with posts, comments, and likes
use_cases.target_user: Mobile and web developers working on social platforms

use_cases.title: Set up local development
use_cases.description: Configure Firebase Emulators to test Auth, Firestore, and Functions without deploying to production
use_cases.target_user: Developers wanting to test Firebase features safely

prompt_templates.title: Basic Security Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Firestore security rules for a collection called 'users' where users can only read and write their own documents
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating basic read/write permissions for authenticated users

prompt_templates.title: Data Model Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Firestore data model for a blog with posts, comments, and likes. Show the collection structure and example documents
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning NoSQL schema for a content platform

prompt_templates.title: Cloud Function Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Firebase Cloud Function that triggers when a new user is created in Authentication and creates a default profile document in Firestore

prompt_templates.title: Cost Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Firestore query pattern and suggest optimizations to reduce read costs: collection('posts').where('author', '==', userId).get()
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimizing database queries for cost efficiency

output_examples.input: Generate security rules for a 'tasks' collection where team members can read all tasks but only edit their own
How should I model a chat application with rooms and messages in Firestore?
output_examples.output:
Recommended structure:
- rooms/{roomId}: { name, createdAt, memberIds[] }
- rooms/{roomId}/messages/{messageId}: { text, senderId, sentAt }

Key decisions:
1. Subcollection for messages keeps room documents small
2. Denormalize memberIds for permission checks
3. Use sentAt for ordering, not document IDs

best_practices: Always design data models around query patterns, not relationships
Write security rules before deploying any collection to production
Use Firebase Emulators for all development and CI testing
anti_patterns: Skipping security rules and relying on client-side validation only
Modeling Firestore like a relational database with deep nesting
Attaching listeners to entire collections without pagination or limits

faq.question: What is the difference between Firestore and Realtime Database?
faq.answer: Firestore is newer with better querying and scalability. Realtime Database is faster for simple sync scenarios. Choose Firestore for most new projects.

faq.question: How do I secure Firebase functions?
faq.answer: Use Firebase Authentication to verify callers. Validate all input data. Avoid exposing service account credentials. Deploy with --public flag only when needed.

faq.question: Can I use Firebase with my own backend?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Firebase Admin SDK on your server to bypass security rules for trusted operations while keeping client-side rules for users.

faq.question: How do I prevent runaway costs with Firestore?
faq.answer: Add limits to queries, use compound indexes wisely, set up billing alerts, and monitor the usage dashboard regularly.

faq.question: What are Firebase Emulators?
faq.answer: Local development servers that simulate Firestore, Auth, Cloud Functions, and other services. Use them to test without incurring costs or affecting production.

faq.question: How do I handle user roles in Firebase?
faq.answer: Store roles in Firestore user documents or use custom claims in the ID token. Check roles in both client security rules and Cloud Functions.
